prompt: A massive photorealistic mosaic portrait filling the entire frame, viewed straight on. When seen as a whole from a distance, the mosaic clearly depicts a solid, unmistakable RED human silhouette or face — the overall image is warm red, orange-red. But every individual tile that makes up the mosaic, when examined closely, is a shade of BLUE — cool blue, steel blue, deep navy. Not a single tile is red. Every tile is blue. Yet together they form something unmistakably red. The contradiction must be visible simultaneously — the whole is red, every part is blue. Some tiles in the foreground should be large enough to clearly see they are blue, while the overall red figure is still clearly visible in the same frame. Mood: clinical, eerie, impossible. No text, no numbers, no labels, no annotations. Photorealistic. Ultra high detail.
visual_logic: A mosaic face that appears entirely red from a distance, yet every individual tile is blue. The group property and individual property are opposite and simultaneously visible — what is true of the whole is true of none of its parts.
